Bash(git commit -m "fix: use correct target value for wheelchair progress (total_requests, not Math.max)

The bug was in this line:
  const target = Math.max(totalRequests, 10);

This meant that when total_requests was 5, Math.max(5, 10) returned 10,
causing the UI to show ""0 / 10"" instead of ""0 / 5"".

Fixed by:
  const target = totalRequests || 10;

This uses the actual total_requests value (5), falling back to 10 only
if there are zero requests.

Also removed debug console.logs from both files.

Issue identified via console logs showing:
- total_requests: 5 ✅
- totalRequests: 5 ✅  
- target: 10 ❌ (should be 5)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>")

Bash(git commit -m "fix: remove Math.max fallback - show exact values from database

Removed all artificial fallbacks (Math.max, || 10) to show real data:

Before:
  const target = totalRequests || 10;  // Would show 0/10 if no requests

After:
  Show exact values: 0/0, 5/5, 10/10, etc.

Changes:
- Removed ''target'' variable entirely
- Calculate percent directly from totalRequests
- Display totalRequests as-is (no minimum of 10)

If there are 0 requests, show 0/0 (not 0/10)
If there are 5 requests, show 0/5 (not 0/10)

Real data, no artificial minimums.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>")

Bash(git commit -m "fix: auto-update volunteer dropdown after part allocation

UX improvement: The volunteer dropdown now updates automatically after
allocating a volunteer to a part, without requiring a page refresh.

Changes:
- Added queryClient.invalidateQueries for ''allocated-contributor-ids''
- This triggers a refetch of allocated volunteers in the current project
- PartAssignmentSelect dropdowns update immediately
- Previously allocated volunteers become disabled in other dropdowns

User experience:
✅ Assign volunteer to part → other dropdowns update instantly
✅ No manual page refresh needed
✅ Previously assigned volunteers disabled in dropdown
✅ Cleaner, more intuitive workflow

Bash(git commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
feat: add reminder email system for assigned parts

New Edge Function (send-reminder-assigned-parts):
- Sends reminder emails to volunteers with parts in "assigned" status
- Indicates parts have not been started yet (not printing/printed/shipped)
- Groups parts by contributor for efficient batch sending
- Template aligned with existing email design (volunteer-welcome, notify-part-allocated)
- Includes portal link, file download links, and print settings warning
- Friendly tone with support message if volunteer cannot complete

Email Features:
- Bell icon in header: "🔔 Lembrete — Peças Atribuídas"
- Lists all assigned parts per volunteer with download links
- Emphasizes updating status to "A Imprimir" in portal
- Blue info box: offers help if volunteer has difficulties
- Consistent branding with logo, green/blue accents, dark footer

ProjectInstancesList.tsx:
- Added Bell icon button next to resend and delete buttons
- handleSendReminders function invokes edge function with project_id
- Loading state with spinner while sending
- Success/error toast notifications
- Only sends to volunteers with status="assigned" in selected project

UX Flow:
1. Admin clicks Bell icon on project detail view
2. System finds all parts with status="assigned" in that project
3. Groups parts by assigned contributor
4. Sends personalized reminder email to each contributor
5. Shows success toast with confirmation

Use Case:
- Remind volunteers who accepted parts but haven''t started printing
- Reduce bottlenecks in project workflow
- Encourage status updates or reallocation if volunteer is blocked
